action.reasoning: Observation: broader type-specific search took ~100 minutes and did not beat the baseline (0.5897 vs 0.5943), so the budget bottleneck is time, not iterations. Analysis: hidden score failures are mainly success-rate failures in rooms/tunnels; the current utility-distance objective generalizes no better than baseline. Before editing, I need inspect the exact current model/config files and identify whether a low-cost planner fix (fallback, local greedy, termination avoidance) is possible. Decision: run a lightweight inspection of current code and saved planner configs only, then make a targeted change in the next iteration rather than launching another long search.
action.command: sed -n '1,260p' model.py && echo '--- train tail ---' && sed -n '1,260p' train.py && echo '--- baseline config ---' && cat checkpoints/baseline/planner_config.json && echo '--- search_v2 config ---' && cat checkpoints/search_v2/planner_config.json
